title: Mike Krzyzewski threw a subtle jab at Bill Belichick in response to new UNC hire
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: UNC hires former Nuggets coach Michael Malone as men's basketball coach, replacing Hubert Davis.
Malone joins Bill Belichick at UNC, both coming from professional sports to lead college teams.
Krzyzewski comments on transition challenges from professional to college coaching after UNC's football struggles under Belichick.

title: Person stabs dog in Milton during alleged attack
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A person stabbed a dog in Milton after allegedly being attacked by the off-leash animal, State Police report.
The incident occurred Monday on Blue Hill River Road, near a local park and recreation area in Milton.
Milton Animal Control is investigating the stabbing, and the injured dog was taken to a nearby veterinarian.

title: Alex Cora reshuffles Red Sox lineup, drops Trevor Story out of No. 2 spot
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Trevor Story moved to No. 5 in the batting order due to a .119 average with no walks and 17 strikeouts.
Masataka Yoshida now bats second; Willson Contreras and Wilyer Abreu were elevated to the No. 3 and No. 4 spots.
Boston Red Sox ranks 26th in MLB walk percentage and 5th in strikeout percentage, averaging 3.3 runs per game.

title: Cleveland radio host says Cavaliers are ‘scared’ of facing the Celtics in the playoffs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: ESPN Cleveland's Andrew Goldhammer claims the Cavaliers want to stay as the No. 4 seed to avoid the Celtics.
The Cavaliers have exited the postseason in the second round for the last two seasons, once against the Celtics in 2024.
The Celtics are currently second seed, having won 10 of their last 12 games, and swept their season series with Cleveland.

title: Where you live in Mass. can shape your electric bill — is yours getting out of control?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts has the highest electricity costs in continental U.S.; costs vary based on utility type and location.
Residents served by municipal utilities pay about half as much for electricity as those with investor-owned utilities.
Switching to municipal utilities involves costly infrastructure purchases; municipal aggregation allows bulk electricity buying by communities.

title: Northeastern students rally over lack of communication after recent violence
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Northeastern students rally for better communication after violence near campus, including a fatal police shooting.
Students criticize the university's delayed alerts, relying more on news reports and police updates for safety information.
Faculty and student leaders urge administration to improve communication on campus safety and related policy issues.

title: BU president pauses signage policy after Pride flag controversy
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: BU President Melissa Gilliam pauses signage policy after Pride flag removal controversy.
Boston University's policy led to Pride flag removals, sparking protests and a petition by students and faculty.
Gilliam emphasized the need for further discussion on university policies and community values.

title: Red Sox complete Sonny Gray trade, send pitching prospect to Cardinals
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Boston Red Sox completed their trade with the Cardinals, sending pitching prospect Patrick Galle to St. Louis.
Patrick Galle, drafted in the 17th round from Ole Miss, had a 4.50 ERA over 7.1 innings with Single A Salem.
Sonny Gray, acquired in November for Galle, Clarke, and Fitts, is scheduled to start against the Brewers on Tuesday.

title: ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island’: Episode 2 recap
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Liz McGraw addresses tension with stepdaughter Brianna and her reluctance to be called 'grandma'.
Ashley Iaconetti shares financial and emotional stresses with Jo-Ellen Tiberi during a playdate.
Rulla Nehme Pontarelli confronts rumors about her husband's alleged affair and Jo-Ellen's involvement.

title: Mass. House to vote on youth social media ban, strict school cellphone ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts House to vote on banning social media for under 14s and imposing school cellphone restrictions.
The legislation mandates parental consent for social media users aged 14-15 and age verification by platforms.
Quincy plans separate all-day school cellphone ban using Yondr pouches for student phones.
